input_summary.complaint: Second-floor bedroom facing a two-way six-lane intersection with braking, horns, motorcycles, and low engine rumble at night.

measurement_plan.objective: Confirm whether low-frequency content is stable enough for a limited quiet-zone policy.
recommended_windows: Collect one quiet baseline when the target noise is absent.
Collect one representative period when the target noise is present.
Measure a 10 to 15 minute nighttime window near the sleep area.
microphone_positions: At the main listener position, such as pillow, desk, or chair.
Near the likely entry path, such as window, wall vent, door gap, or facade.
observations_to_log: source notes such as traffic, aircraft, machinery, voices, or unknown
user impact such as sleep, focus, stress, or safety concern
safety_notes: Keep raw audio local by default; retain only derived acoustic features in the report.
Record whether alarms, sirens, smoke detectors, or urgent speech must remain audible.
Before any active playback, repeat measurements with calibrated microphones and output limits.

notes: No measured features were provided; conclusion is based on complaint and scenario templates.

noise_profile.source_model: outdoor traffic transmitted through window and room reflections

near_field_anc.reason: Stable low-frequency components may be reduced near a pillow with calibrated microphones, speakers, and local DSP.

passive_insulation.reason: Passive controls reduce airborne and reflected noise without depending on phase cancellation.

masking_sound.reason: Masking can reduce perceived residual events, but it must not cover safety-critical sounds or replace hearing protection.

hearing_protection.reason: Certified protection may be needed for high-frequency or occupational exposure; for sleep it is optional only if safety alerts remain audible.

recommended_controls.reason: Passive treatment works across frequency bands and does not depend on a clean phase model.

recommended_controls.target: alarms, sirens, smoke detectors, urgent speech

recommended_controls.reason: Stable low-frequency components may be reduced near a pillow with calibrated microphones, speakers, and local DSP.
High-frequency impulsive noise should be handled with passive, engineering, or certified protective controls.

blocked_controls.reason: Open-air whole-room cancellation is not realistic for reflected environmental noise.
Ultrasonic transducers do not directly cancel audible noise and can introduce distortion or safety concerns.

anc_policy.reason: Enable only as a limited low-frequency local quiet-zone strategy.
anc_policy.microphone_strategy: reference microphone near likely entry path and error microphone near sleep position

analysis_conclusion.summary: Limited local ANC may be considered only after measurement confirms the low-frequency target.
analysis_conclusion.rationale: Stable low-frequency components may be reduced near a pillow with calibrated microphones, speakers, and local DSP.
analysis_conclusion.next_step: Run the measurement plan, then validate calibration before any active playback.

rules: Do not claim medical diagnosis or guaranteed sleep improvement.
Preserve alarms, sirens, smoke detectors, and urgent speech.
Do not retain raw audio by default.
Require calibrated hardware and conservative output limits before real playback.

notes: Default mode keeps only derived acoustic features.
Raw recordings should not be uploaded or stored unless the user explicitly opts in.

caveats: This plan is not a medical diagnosis.
A laptop speaker is not a valid actuator for real traffic or aircraft ANC.
Real deployment requires calibrated microphones, speakers, and local DSP.
Impulsive events may be better handled by passive controls and alert-aware policies.
